D7 Print L3 Apply Reference

Four-Colour Separation

a full-colour image is printed from four single-ink plates — cyan, magenta, yellow, and black — each a halftone on its own screen angle

To print colour, the image is separated into four ink channels. Cyan, magenta, and yellow subtract red, green, and blue; black (K) deepens shadows and sharpens text. Each plate prints as a halftone on its own angle (C 15°, K 45°, M 75°, Y 90°) so the four overlay into a rosette rather than a moiré.

Isolate each plate to see which inks carry which colours — magenta and yellow build the reds, cyan and yellow the greens — then return to the composite. This is a simple GCR; production prepress uses tuned black-generation curves and ink limits.
